LAW AND MOTION, 302, PETITIONER LUXFER-GTM TECHOLOGIES, LLC'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS DENIED. LUXFER FAILED TO SATISFY ITS BURDEN THAT THERE IS AN ARBITRATION AGREEMENT COVERING THE 152 PODS THAT IT CLAIMS RESPONDENT HICOR TECHNOLOGIES, INC. WAS REQUIRED TO PURCHASE AND PAY FOR. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE A FORM OF ORDER THAT TRACKS THE TENTATIVE RULING VERBATIM AND SHOW COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
ORDER DENYING PETITIONER'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION

Off Calendar Oct-25-2017 Inactivate Attorney

PRO HAC VICE RENEWAL FEE STATUS FOR ATTORNEY REBECCA F. BRIGGS
Off Calendar Sep-13-2017 Continued To Oct-25-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Petitioner Luxfer-Gtm Technologies Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Oct-27-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Petitioner Luxfer-GTM Techologies, LLC's petition to compel arbitration is denied. Luxfer failed to satisfy its burden that there is an arbitration agreement covering the 152 pods that it claims respondent Hicor Technologies, Inc. was required to purchase and pay for. The parties' agreement concerning the 200 pods is contained in the letter agreement dated September 26, 2016 sent by Luxfer and signed by Hicor. Luxfer's March 4, 2013 invoice and attached standard terms and conditions was not signed by Hicor and was accepted by Hicor only as to the 15 pods that were covered by that invoice, which are not part of the 152 pods in dispute between the parties. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim and show compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
